element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core vehicle for majority of campaign; designed for speed and combat
Unlimited ammo, lowest damage output; baseline weapon
More powerful than machine guns; limited carry capacity; weapon selection strategy required
Most deadly weapon; severely limited ammunition; tactical deployment required
Airlifting rescued friendlies to landing zones; provides armor replenishment incentive
Helicopter fuel depletes steadily; collected via fuel barrels; resource constraint
Depletes on enemy contact; replenished via power-ups or rescued friendlies
Heavier, slower design; specialized for large-scale rescue operations
Alternative vehicle; stolen and piloted as situational replacement
Player leaves helicopter; armed with rifle and missile launcher; internal environment navigation

typical_relief_triggers: Successful rescue airlifts to landing zones
Collecting fuel barrels to restore resource
Collecting ammo crates to restore weapon capacity
typical_tension_triggers: Armor reaching critical levels from enemy fire
Ammunition running dry on preferred weapons
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Resource management loop with continuous pressure; tension maintained through depletion mechanics (fuel, armor, ammo) requiring active scavenging and rescue completion for relief

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible introduction; single helicopter type in Hawaii with basic objective structure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Expanded vehicle options (Blackhawke, GAV); introduction of on-foot missions; geographic progression through Mexico and San Francisco
difficulty_curve.late_game: Superweapon deployment; defensive/containment missions in New York and Las Vegas; increased enemy density and complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign progresses across North American locations: Hawaii → Mexico → San Francisco → New York → Las Vegas; each region associated with mission objectives tied to Malone's superweapon construction
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 (Hawaii - mirror theft interception and surgeon rescue)

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Mixed combat and rescue mission structure
notable_level_patterns.description: Levels consist of multiple missions balancing destruction objectives (enemy weapons/installations) with secondary rescue/capture objectives; rewards armor recovery for completing rescues
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Hawaii level (mirror theft + plastic surgeon rescue dual objectives)

notable_level_patterns.description: Specific levels enforce use of alternative vehicles; Blackhawke used in early levels for large-scale rescues; GAV stolen in Level 3; on-foot sections scattered throughout campaign
Plot progression directly influences threat escalation; Malone's weapon fires increasingly, forcing containment/rescue missions before final infiltration
notable_level_patterns.example_level: New York (post-weapon deployment); Las Vegas (facility infiltration finale)
